tools.purpose: Organisational folder owning two sub-concerns: database migration tooling (tools/migrate) and a bash readiness helper (wait-for-compose.sh). Acts as the operational boundary between schema authorship (Ent/Atlas) and runtime startup; nothing in tools/ contains business logic.

patterns.name: wait-for-compose health check
patterns.description: wait-for-compose.sh polls docker compose container health status (healthy/running) before CI steps proceed. Uses `docker compose ps -q` + `docker inspect` in a 60-attempt loop with 2s sleep.

key_file_guides.role: CI readiness gate: blocks until listed docker compose services are healthy or running. Used by Makefile targets before running tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Containers with no healthcheck fall through to state-based check (running/restarting/created). Exit 1 on unhealthy or timeout after 120s.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain imports to anything under tools/
Hand-editing tools/migrate/migrations/ — Atlas owns the chain and atlas.sum will fail CI
Calling atlas migrate diff expecting new ent.View schemas to appear — views require viewgen
Using ORM (Ent/GORM) inside migration stop tests — raw *sql.DB is required

decisions.decision: wait-for-compose.sh is a standalone bash script rather than a Go helper
decisions.rationale: It must run before any Go binary is available; bash docker-inspect polling has no runtime dependencies.
